generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element within the incubator enclosure responsible for generating and maintaining target temperature levels through closed-loop control."
]

instance IncubatorFan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Air circulation component ensuring uniform temperature distribution within the styrofoam enclosure through controlled rotational speed adjustments."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller executing Python-based control algorithms and interfacing with sensors/actuators via GPIO interfaces for real-time system regulation."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring internal incubator temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy, deployed within the styrofoam enclosure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based analytical model representing heat transfer dynamics within the incubator enclosure, calibrated using differential equations and real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonCalibrationEngine
]

instance EnvironmentalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational model accounting for external factors affecting incubator performance including ambient temperature variations and humidity effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonCalibrationEngine
]

instance IncubatorStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series dataset containing real-time temperature measurements, calibration parameters, and system performance metrics for state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonCalibrationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PythonCalibrationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scipy-based parameter estimation algorithm implementing least squares and Bayesian methods to iteratively refine thermal and environmental models using real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicModelCalibrationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance StateEstimationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter-based sensor fusion system reconciling discrepancies between model predictions and observed physical behavior for accurate state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceMonitoringService, SupervisoryControlService
]

instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asynchronous message broker facilitating reliable data transmission between Raspberry Pi controller and Digital Twin components via co-located RabbitMQ server."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataAcquisitionService
]

instance RealTimeDataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously collecting and processing sensor data from the Physical Twin for immediate use in model calibration and state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SensorDataFeed
]

instance SensorDataFeed : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of validated sensor readings from the Physical Twin used as input for all calibration and monitoring services."
]

// C6: Services
instance DynamicModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously updating physics-based models by adjusting parameters (heatbed efficiency, fan distribution) based on monitored deviations from expected behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyMetrics, CalibrationAlerts
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring service comparing model predictions with sensor readings to identify equipment failures (sensor drift, heating element degradation)."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionAlerts, EquipmentHealthMetrics
]

instance PerformanceMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service tracking operational performance metrics including thermal stability, energy consumption, and deviation from target setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalPerformanceInsights, EnergyEfficiencyRecommendations
]

instance SupervisoryControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Decision support service generating control signals for Raspberry Pi to adjust actuators (fan speed, heatbed power) based on state estimation outputs."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentRecommendations, SafetyThresholdViolations
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ModelAccuracyMetrics : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative measures of model calibration quality including parameter estimation accuracy and prediction error margins derived from real-time validation."
]

instance CalibrationAlerts : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Notifications indicating successful model recalibration events or parameter adjustment thresholds being met during iterative refinement cycles."
]

instance FaultDetectionAlerts : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time alerts triggered when detected deviations exceed predefined safety thresholds, including specific failure modes (e.g., heating element failure)."
]

instance EquipmentHealthMetrics : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive health status indicators for all physical components including degradation rates, operational lifetimes, and maintenance recommendations."
]

instance OperationalPerformanceInsights : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics outputs showing current operational conditions, performance trends, and recommendations for optimizing energy consumption."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendations : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Parameter adjustment suggestions for heatbed/fan operation to minimize energy consumption while maintaining target temperature stability."
]

instance ControlAdjustmentRecommendations : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control signals specifying optimal actuator settings (fan speed percentages, heatbed power levels) to maintain system stability."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyThresholdViolations : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Immediate corrective actions required when system parameters exceed predefined safety limits, triggering emergency shutdown protocols."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains IncubatorEnclosure, ThermalControlSystem
]

instance IncubatorEnclosure : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box providing thermal insulation and physical containment for all internal components including sensors and actuators."
]

instance ThermalControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, IncubatorFan, InternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Closed-loop control regime integrating all physical components to maintain precise temperature regulation within ±0.5°C tolerance."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **bang-bang controller** to generate control commands for the Physical Twin, transmitting commands via the same **RabbitMQ** message broker used for data ingestion. Command formats are not explicitly detailed, but the system includes **human-in-the-loop validation** for critical adjustments. Feedback mechanisms are implicit through the **monitoring and calibration algorithms** that detect performance deviations (e.g., temperature deviations) and trigger corrective actions. No explicit conflict resolution, authentication, or command execution confirmation mechanisms are described beyond the controller’s binary output (on/off)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates within a closed-loop temporal framework where **data acquisition** from the Physical Twin (PT) is periodically executed by low-level drivers (e.g., temperature sensors) to feed real-time operational data into the Digital Twin. The **calibration algorithms** periodically update the system models (e.g., Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environmental models) using the latest sensor readings, though explicit temporal frequencies (e.g., milliseconds, seconds) are not specified in the provided documents. The **monitoring phase**—which checks for safe and optimal performance—relies on this updated data, with violations triggering immediate human or algorithmic intervention. Latency requirements are implied to be **minimal** to ensure real-time responsiveness, though no quantitative thresholds (e.g., sub-second delays) are defined. Temporal granularity is dictated by the **periodicity of sensor readings** and the **frequency of model recalibration**, both of which are critical for maintaining synchronization between the PT and DT. The system lacks explicit mention of asynchronous or event-driven time scales, focusing instead on a **synchronous, periodic update cycle** tied to sensor-driven data ingestion and model refinement."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT system follows a **modular architecture pattern** where core components—**data collection, models, and algorithms**—are tightly integrated to enable end-to-end functionality. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to refine models of the CPS and environment, followed by **monitoring services** that validate system performance against safety and optimality criteria. Resource management is implicit, with algorithms dynamically manipulating data and models to generate insights (e.g., fault detection, state estimation). System-wide coordination is achieved through **closed-loop feedback mechanisms**, where violations trigger human or automated interventions (e.g., parameter adjustments, reconfigurations). No explicit mention of orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or middleware (e.g., MQTT, Kafka) is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  The document outlines key aspects of the Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle, though not explicitly segmented into formal stages. Key inferred phases and characteristics include:

  1. **Development Phase**:
     - **Representation Types**: The lifecycle begins with the creation of abstract models (e.g., CAD models for physical design, dynamic models for behavior analysis) to understand specific aspects of the Physical Twin (PT).
     - **Integration**: Models are not discarded post-deployment but integrated into the DT framework, combining **data** (sensor-collected), **models** (knowledge of PT and environment), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, optimizers).
     - **Transition Management**: The transition from standalone models to DT involves embedding them into a unified framework that supports real-time monitoring, simulation, and optimization.

  2. **Operational Phase**:
     - **Dynamic Updates**: The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models with real-time data (e.g., sensor inputs) to refine parameter estimates of the PT and its environment.
     - **Monitoring and Control**: Monitors assess system performance (safety/optimality). Violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions (e.g., adjusting the PT).
     - **Lifecycle Governance**: Implicit governance involves iterative feedback loops between the DT and PT, ensuring alignment between virtual and physical systems. The document emphasizes the DT's role in **adding value** to the PT, suggesting continuous refinement and adaptation.

  **Not explicitly detailed**: Formal governance frameworks (e.g., roles, compliance), phase-specific milestones, or decommissioning processes.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The fidelity and validity of the Digital Twin system are ensured through a **calibration methodology** using a **non-linear least squares solver**, which minimizes residual errors between simulated and experimental data trajectories. The **validation process** involves comparing the resulting model behavior with real-time data from the Physical Twin (PT) to assess predictive accuracy. Uncertainty quantification is implicitly addressed through the calibration process, though no explicit statistical analysis of sensor noise or periodic recalibration protocols is mentioned. The **quality assurance** is primarily focused on the initial calibration experiment to achieve minimal residual error, with no detailed descriptions of ongoing error detection, correction mechanisms, or threshold-based alerts (e.g., ±1 °C deviations) provided in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical connection relies on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** as the primary node, utilizing **Wi-Fi and Bluetooth** for communication with the Physical Twin. The system employs **RabbitMQ** as the message broker for both data ingestion and command transmission, with no mention of additional protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus). Network topology is not detailed beyond the Pi’s local connectivity, and bandwidth/latency requirements are unspecified. Security protocols are not addressed; reliability and redundancy mechanisms are absent from the description."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed **locally on a Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, with the server and message broker (RabbitMQ) running **on the same device**. No cloud/edge hybrid or on-premise infrastructure is mentioned; computational and storage requirements are not quantified. Scalability mechanisms are not discussed, and the deployment model is **single-node** with no redundancy or high-availability features. Availability/reliability assurance is not addressed beyond the system’s local operation."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) incorporates a **safe protocol** embedded within the low-level driver to ensure operational integrity of the Physical Twin (PT). Monitoring components within the DT continuously assess system performance to verify **safety and optimality**, detecting deviations from expected behavior. When violations are identified—such as unsafe or suboptimal conditions—the system triggers **corrective actions**, which may be automated or human-intervened. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to refine models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, ensuring parameter estimates align with real-time PT behavior. While explicit **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems** are not detailed, the framework inherently relies on **monitoring-based safety checks** to mitigate risks associated with system failures or malfunctions. The DT’s architecture prioritizes **fail-safe mechanisms** by leveraging real-time data and model updates to prevent cascading failures or unsafe states in the PT."
]
